scenario_name: Scenario 20 — High-Stakes Implementation Under Incomplete Spec
justification: AC1-AC3 formula exactly correct. AC4 uses Math.floor(x*100)/100 (floor rounding, not round-half-up). AC5 zero returns 0. Unspecified edge cases documented in deviations with clear justification. Confidence section marks AC1-AC5 as high confidence, floating-point precision as low confidence. Tests cover AC3 exact value ($66.50), floor rounding (confirms $33.33 not $33.34), zero case, and boundary cases. Financial precision concern noted in low_confidence_areas.

observations.text: Floor rounding explicitly confirmed with $33.33 vs $33.34 test case — demonstrates understanding of overpay risk
Deviations clearly document the decision to leave negative/over-100% inputs unguarded, with rationale (masking upstream bugs)
14 tests including boundary cases (sub-dollar, $1M, 100% fee, 100% share) show thorough coverage
Floating-point precision concern noted for large amounts with irrational shares — exactly the kind of financial awareness expected

Confidence calibration is slightly off — edge case behavior (negative amounts, >1.0 percentages) should appear explicitly in low_confidence_areas rather than only in deviations. The rubric expects edge case behaviors at LOWER confidence with notes, and while the deviations section covers this, the confidence section's low_confidence_areas only mentions floating-point, not the unguarded input validation decision
Chose not to block implementation over unspecified edge cases — correctly prioritized delivering the core spec
